clusters.title: 'Extremely embarrassing' for govt but no reprimand for Ben-Gvir over flotilla video
clusters.neutral_headline: How Ben-Gvir’s flotilla video shattered Israel’s multimillion ‘Hasbara’
bullet_summary: Ben Gvir’s video of bound flotilla activists showed Israel without the mask
Three activists were taken to hospital as result of Israeli violence, lawyers representing the group said

items.title: 'Extremely embarrassing' for govt but no reprimand for Ben-Gvir over flotilla video
items.standfirst: Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has called the treatment of detained Gaza flotilla activists by National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir “not in line with Israel's values and norms”, after a video of Ben-Gvir taunting the detainees sparked international outcry. However, while “extremely embarrassing” for the Israeli government, there has been little in the way of reprimand for Ben-Gvir, says France 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky.
items.content: Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has called the treatment of detained Gaza flotilla activists by National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir “not in line with Israel's values and norms”, after a video of Ben-Gvir taunting the detainees sparked international outcry. However, while “extremely embarrassing” for the Israeli government, there has been little in the way of reprimand for Ben-Gvir, says France 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky.

items.content: Activists have begun to leave Israel after international outrage over their treatment in detention.

items.title: How Ben-Gvir’s flotilla video shattered Israel’s multimillion ‘Hasbara’

items.title: UK summons Israel’s chargé d’affaires over video of minister taunting activists
items.standfirst: Foreign Office denounces treatment of Gaza flotilla activists as outcry over Itamar Ben-Gvir’s video escalatesThe UK has summoned Israel’s chargé d’affaires as international outrage escalates over a video posted by the national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, in which he is seen taunting activists detained after a Gaza-bound flotilla was intercepted.The global outcry continued as Israel began releasing hundreds of the activists who attempted to breach Israel’s naval blockade of Gaza. Authorities are in the process of deporting them, according to a legal organisation working with the flotilla. Continue reading...

Authorities are in the process of deporting them, according to a legal organisation working with the flotilla. Continue reading...

items.content: In a video released by his office, National Security Minister Ben-Gvir taunts zip-tied activists, causing condemnation.

items.title: Israel: Video of security minister taunting flotilla activists sparks outrage
items.standfirst: Israel's security minister sparked outrage at home and abroad after he showed himself taunting activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla bound and forced to their knees following their arrest by Israeli troops. Foreign ministers from many countries have denounced a "monstrous" treatment, while Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has denounced actions "not in line with Israel's values".
items.content: Israel's security minister sparked outrage at home and abroad after he showed himself taunting activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla bound and forced to their knees following their arrest by Israeli troops. Foreign ministers from many countries have denounced a "monstrous" treatment, while Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has denounced actions "not in line with Israel's values".

items.title: Gaza flotilla activists to be deported from Israel as outrage grows over treatment
items.standfirst: Gaza flotilla activists who were detained by Israel and later pinned to the ground to the taunts of Israel’s far-right national security minister have been released from prison and will be deported to Turkey on Thursday, officials said.
items.content: Gaza flotilla activists who were detained by Israel and later pinned to the ground to the taunts of Israel’s far-right national security minister have been released from prison and will be deported to Turkey on Thursday, officials said.

items.title: Backlash against Israel's 'racist thug' Ben-Gvir over flotilla video
items.standfirst: PRESS REVIEW – Thursday, May 21: There's outrage and condemnation in the international papers over a video showing Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir taunting detained flotilla activists. One Israeli editor says he should be fired for his video. Also: the US indicts former Cuban president Raul Castro – is this a prelude for a Maduro-style US military operation in Cuba? Plus, a study shows that cows can recognise familiar faces – they have "déjà moo"!
items.content: PRESS REVIEW – Thursday, May 21: There's outrage and condemnation in the international papers over a video showing Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir taunting detained flotilla activists. One Israeli editor says he should be fired for his video. Also: the US indicts former Cuban president Raul Castro – is this a prelude for a Maduro-style US military operation in Cuba? Plus, a study shows that cows can recognise familiar faces – they have "déjà moo"!

items.title: Far-right Israeli minister condemned for taunting handcuffed Gaza flotilla activists

items.content: France and Italy are among the countries that have criticised a video showing Itamar Ben-Gvir taunting dozens of activists detained at an Israeli port.

items.title: Israeli security minister stirs diplomatic outrage with flotilla activist abuse video
items.standfirst: Far-right figure Itamar Ben-Gvir shares footage of himself taunting bound international detaineesIsrael’s far-right national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, has sparked a diplomatic crisis by publishing footage of Israeli security forces abusing international activists who were detained as they tried to sail to Gaza with aid.Three activists were taken to hospital as result of Israeli violence, lawyers representing the group said. They were subsequently discharged. Dozens of others have suspected broken ribs, resulting in breathing problems. Continue reading...

They were subsequently discharged. Dozens of others have suspected broken ribs, resulting in breathing problems. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Israeli police have been filmed forcing activists to kneel on the ground with their hands tied behind their backs while the country's national security minister tells them they should be imprisoned for a long time.

clusters.title: French court finds Air France, Airbus guilty over 2009 Rio-Paris crash
clusters.neutral_headline: Air France and Airbus found guilty of manslaughter over 2009 plane crash
bullet_summary: The companies were found guilty by a French court over an air disaster which killed 228 people
Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: French court finds Air France, Airbus guilty over 2009 Rio-Paris crash
items.standfirst: A Paris appeals court Thursday found Air France and Airbus guilty of involuntary manslaughter over the 2009 crash of a Rio-Paris flight that killed 228 people, the worst disaster in France's aviation history.
items.content: A Paris appeals court Thursday found Air France and Airbus guilty of involuntary manslaughter over the 2009 crash of a Rio-Paris flight that killed 228 people, the worst disaster in France's aviation history.

items.title: Air France and Airbus found guilty of manslaughter over 2009 plane crash
items.standfirst: The companies were found guilty by a French court over an air disaster which killed 228 people.
items.content: The companies were found guilty by a French court over an air disaster which killed 228 people.

items.title: Air France and Airbus guilty of corporate manslaughter for 2009 plane crash
items.standfirst: Verdict is latest legal milestone over France’s worst ever air disasterA Paris appeals court has found Airbus and Air France guilty of corporate manslaughter over the 2009 Rio-Paris plane crash that killed 228 passengers and crew in France’s worst air disaster.The verdict is the latest milestone in a legal marathon involving two of France’s most emblematic companies and families of the mainly French, Brazilian and German victims. Continue reading...
items.content: Verdict is latest legal milestone over France’s worst ever air disasterA Paris appeals court has found Airbus and Air France guilty of corporate manslaughter over the 2009 Rio-Paris plane crash that killed 228 passengers and crew in France’s worst air disaster.The verdict is the latest milestone in a legal marathon involving two of France’s most emblematic companies and families of the mainly French, Brazilian and German victims. Continue reading...

items.title: French court finds Air France, Airbus guilty of involuntary manslaughter over 2009 Rio-Paris crash
items.standfirst: Air France and Airbus were on Thursday found guilty of involuntary manslaughter by a Paris appeals court in connection to the 2009 crash of a Rio-Paris flight that killed 228 people, the worst disaster in France's aviation history.
items.content: Air France and Airbus were on Thursday found guilty of involuntary manslaughter by a Paris appeals court in connection to the 2009 crash of a Rio-Paris flight that killed 228 people, the worst disaster in France's aviation history.

items.title: Verdict due in Rio-Paris plane crash appeals case
items.standfirst: A Paris appeals court will issue a verdict on Thursday on the 2009 crash of an Air France passenger jet after the airline and planemaker Airbus faced trial over ​corporate manslaughter. Relatives of ‌some of the 228 passengers and crew who died when the Airbus A330 ⁠vanished in darkness during an Atlantic storm are expected in court following a 17-year legal battle to pinpoint blame for France's worst air disaster.
items.content: A Paris appeals court will issue a verdict on Thursday on the 2009 crash of an Air France passenger jet after the airline and planemaker Airbus faced trial over ​corporate manslaughter. Relatives of ‌some of the 228 passengers and crew who died when the Airbus A330 ⁠vanished in darkness during an Atlantic storm are expected in court following a 17-year legal battle to pinpoint blame for France's worst air disaster.

clusters.title: US indicts former Cuban president Raúl Castro as it seeks to oust regime | First Thing
clusters.neutral_headline: US indicts former Cuban president Raúl Castro as it seeks to oust regime
bullet_summary: The US ambassador to Israel, Mike Huckabee, one of the country’s staunchest allies, described Ben-Gvir’s behaviour as “despicable” and said the minister had “betrayed the dignity of his nation”
The ​United States announced murder charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro on Wednesday, a major escalation in Washington's pressure campaign against the island's communist government

items.title: US indicts former Cuban president Raúl Castro as it seeks to oust regime | First Thing
items.standfirst: Charges filed against Raúl Castro for allegedly shooting down planes in 1996. Plus, US employers spend more than $1.5bn a year to fight labor unionsDon’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up hereGood morning.On Wednesday, the US issued a federal criminal indictment against Raúl Castro, Cuba’s former president, and five others, in a significant escalation of the Trump administration’s campaign to oust the country’s communist regime.What are the details? Castro, 94, was charged with conspiracy to kill US nationals, four counts of murder and two counts of destruction of aircraft related to an incident in 1996 – in which four men were killed by the Cuban military, when two small planes were shot down during a humanitarian mission in the Florida straits.How has Cuba reacted? Miguel Díaz-Canel, the Cuban president, condemned the indictment as a political stunt that sought only to “justify the folly of a military aggression against Cuba”.What does the video show? Images of dozens of men and women kneeling in rows, with their foreheads to the ground and their hands zip-tied behind their back. Ben-Gvir posted it on his social media account. He appears waving an Israeli flag, mocking and taunting the detainees.What are global leaders saying? The US ambassador to Israel, Mike Huckabee, one of the country’s staunchest allies, described Ben-Gvir’s behaviour as “despicable” and said the minister had “betrayed the dignity of his nation”. Others criticizing the video were Italy, Spain, the European Council, Australia, New Zealand, the UK and others. The Spanish foreign minister called the treatment “monstrous, disgraceful and inhumane”. Continue reading...
items.content: Charges filed against Raúl Castro for allegedly shooting down planes in 1996. Plus, US employers spend more than $1.5bn a year to fight labor unionsDon’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up hereGood morning.On Wednesday, the US issued a federal criminal indictment against Raúl Castro, Cuba’s former president, and five others, in a significant escalation of the Trump administration’s campaign to oust the country’s communist regime.What are the details? Castro, 94, was charged with conspiracy to kill US nationals, four counts of murder and two counts of destruction of aircraft related to an incident in 1996 – in which four men were killed by the Cuban military, when two small planes were shot down during a humanitarian mission in the Florida straits.How has Cuba reacted? Miguel Díaz-Canel, the Cuban president, condemned the indictment as a political stunt that sought only to “justify the folly of a military aggression against Cuba”.What does the video show? Images of dozens of men and women kneeling in rows, with their foreheads to the ground and their hands zip-tied behind their back. Ben-Gvir posted it on his social media account. He appears waving an Israeli flag, mocking and taunting the detainees.What are global leaders saying? The US ambassador to Israel, Mike Huckabee, one of the country’s staunchest allies, described Ben-Gvir’s behaviour as “despicable” and said the minister had “betrayed the dignity of his nation”. Others criticizing the video were Italy, Spain, the European Council, Australia, New Zealand, the UK and others. The Spanish foreign minister called the treatment “monstrous, disgraceful and inhumane”. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump escalates pressure on Cuba as US charges Raul Castro with murder
items.standfirst: The ​United States announced murder charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro on Wednesday, a major escalation in Washington's pressure campaign against the island's communist government. The indictment marks a new low in relations between the longtime Cold War rivalsand comes as U.S. President Donald Trump is pushingfor regime change in Cuba, where Castro's communists have been in charge since his late brother Fidel Castro led a revolution in 1959.
items.content: The ​United States announced murder charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro on Wednesday, a major escalation in Washington's pressure campaign against the island's communist government. The indictment marks a new low in relations between the longtime Cold War rivalsand comes as U.S. President Donald Trump is pushingfor regime change in Cuba, where Castro's communists have been in charge since his late brother Fidel Castro led a revolution in 1959.

items.title: US indicts former Cuban president Raúl Castro as it seeks to oust regime

Continue reading...

items.title: Record 274 climbers scale Nepal's side of Mount Everest in single day
items.standfirst: A record 274 climbers scaled the Nepal side of Mount Everest in a single day, officials said. They took advantage of clear weather.
items.content: A record 274 climbers scaled the Nepal side of Mount Everest in a single day, officials said. They took advantage of clear weather.

items.title: Record 274 climbers summit Everest from Nepalese side in single day
items.standfirst: Climbers take advantage of clear weather after threat of ice fall on normal route delayed start of spring season A record 274 climbers have reached the summit of Mount Everest from the Nepalese side in a single day after a spring season that started late because of the threat of ice fall on the normal tourist route.The climbers took advantage of the clear weather on Wednesday, said Rishi Ram Bhandari, of the Expedition Operators Association Nepal. Continue reading...
items.content: Climbers take advantage of clear weather after threat of ice fall on normal route delayed start of spring season A record 274 climbers have reached the summit of Mount Everest from the Nepalese side in a single day after a spring season that started late because of the threat of ice fall on the normal tourist route.The climbers took advantage of the clear weather on Wednesday, said Rishi Ram Bhandari, of the Expedition Operators Association Nepal. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Beyond Plastics says tracked Starbucks cups didn’t reach a recycling facility
clusters.neutral_headline: Beyond Plastics says tracked Starbucks cups didn’t reach a recycling facility
bullet_summary: A three-month investigation found that none of the tracked Starbucks cold-beverage cups placed in in-store recycling bins ended up at a recycling facility
Reported by Reuters Top News, Guardian Business

items.title: Beyond Plastics says tracked Starbucks cups didn’t reach a recycling facility
items.standfirst: A three-month investigation found that none of the tracked Starbucks cold-beverage cups placed in in-store recycling bins ended up at a recycling facility. Instead, the cups were tracked to landfills, incinerators, waste-transfer stations, and material recovery facilities, raising questions about Starbucks’ “widely recyclable” claim. [beyondplastics.org]
items.content: A three-month investigation found that none of the tracked Starbucks cold-beverage cups placed in in-store recycling bins ended up at a recycling facility. Instead, the cups were tracked to landfills, incinerators, waste-transfer stations, and material recovery facilities, raising questions about Starbucks’ “widely recyclable” claim. [beyondplastics.org]

items.title: Starbucks does not recycle plastic cups it claims are ‘widely recyclable’, report says
items.standfirst: Watchdog group glued trackers to 53 of the chain’s cups across nine states and found none ended up at a recycling facilityIf you attach a GPS tracker to a “widely recyclable” plastic Starbucks cup and drop it in an in-store recycling bin, you might expect it to end up in a recycling plant, but the environmental watchdog organization Beyond Plastics says that’s not the case in a new report.Starbucks announced that their plastic cups were now considered “widely recyclable” earlier this year, according to How2Recycle, a group affiliated with the consumer packaging industry that helps private companies label their packaging with recycling options. The coffee giant touted the achievement as a “big milestone, with huge impact”. Continue reading...
items.content: Watchdog group glued trackers to 53 of the chain’s cups across nine states and found none ended up at a recycling facilityIf you attach a GPS tracker to a “widely recyclable” plastic Starbucks cup and drop it in an in-store recycling bin, you might expect it to end up in a recycling plant, but the environmental watchdog organization Beyond Plastics says that’s not the case in a new report.Starbucks announced that their plastic cups were now considered “widely recyclable” earlier this year, according to How2Recycle, a group affiliated with the consumer packaging industry that helps private companies label their packaging with recycling options. The coffee giant touted the achievement as a “big milestone, with huge impact”. Continue reading...
